summary: Adversarial law review of the constitution TEXT (hostile-maintainer lens; verifier's cite/suite pass not re-done — concur PASS). ONE decision-forcing structural loophole: L1 — VII.2's entrenchment protects Art. II + the secret floor but is NOT self-entrenching and does not protect Art. III/VI/VII, enabling a technically-compliant two-step amendment (weaken VII.2 or Art. III first, then Art. II / the secret floor) — exactly the Preamble's own threat model. Founding is the uniquely cheap moment to close it. Seven interpretive advisories: A-chapeau (Art. II chapeau contradicts II.5/II.6 + wrong '(Article VII)' cross-ref — should be CF1), A-substantial ('substantial' undefined — add when-in-doubt rule), A-precedence-tiebreak (schemas vs registries intra-rung), A-mode-hatch (V.2 any-override vs V.6 mode stand-down unreconciled), A-discoverability (read-before-change duty aspirational for a fresh session — CLAUDE.md/DMC.md carry no breadcrumb by dangling-ref design; record tradeoff), A-lexeme-self-enforcement (VII.4 honor-system — require grep evidence at the amendment gate), A-ci-red-tier (III.4 freeze should bind to the BLOCKING tier, not the advisory replay). Security: runtime floor untouched; the floor's CONSTITUTIONAL protection inherits the L1 gap.

criteria_checked.note: pointer surfaces not auto-injected; MUST aspirational

blockers.statement: VII.2 entrenchment not self-entrenching; does not protect Art. III (human gate + non-authoring review), Art. VI, or Art. VII itself — two-step technically-compliant amendment defeats 'what may NEVER change' incl. the secret floor's constitutional protection.
blockers.suggested_fix: Self-referential + procedural entrenchment: 'No amendment may weaken Article II, the secret-protection floor, THIS clause and Article VII's entrenchment, Article VI's precedence, or Article III's human-gate and non-authoring critic/verifier requirements.'

advisories.statement: Art. II chapeau: scope hygiene-milestone/never-in-feature language to the byte-frozen surfaces; correct '(Article VII)' to CF1; let II.5/II.6 carry their own procedures.
Define/cite the 'substantial' threshold or add 'when in doubt, the cycle applies.'
Add intra-rung tiebreak: schema contracts control shape; registries control instances/values; on shape conflict the schema controls.
Reconcile V.2 with V.6: state whether a gate-standing-down mode flip is V.2-governed or a designed hatch bounded by the deny floor, and what authorizes it.

Read-before-change duty aspirational for a fresh session (only AGENTS.md/CONTEXT_MAP pointers, not auto-injected; CLAUDE.md silent by dangling-ref design) — record the tradeoff or add a non-dangling breadcrumb later (CLAUDE.md is out of THIS plan's scope; future-amendment candidate).
VII.4: require the lexeme-grep output as an evidence artifact at the amendment commit gate.
III.4: bind the freeze to 'CI red on a BLOCKING-in-CI check' (advisory replay red does not trigger it).
Runtime secret floor untouched and correctly named un-weakenable; the constitutional-protection gap is L1's, closed by the L1 fix.
